source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Dropped Drill Pipe, Thunderhorse PDQ

actions_taken: Injured Floor Hand reported to Medic
Injured Floor Hand sent for further medical evaluation
BSEE on-site investigation conducted on March 1, 2023
In-person meeting requested between BSEE, BP, and Valaris on March 3, 2023
event.summary: On February 18, 2023, at 21:27 hours, while performing pulling out of hole (POOH) operations on the TA004 well at MC 778, the Driller inadvertently opened the pipe elevators before the Pipe Racking System (PRS) could secure the 13th stand of 4-1/2 inch drill pipe. The stand fell across the rig floor, striking the auxiliary PRS hoist and falling within close proximity of three Floor Hands. One Floor Hand slipped on the deck while evacuating and sustained injuries to his knee and hip, requiring medical evacuation.
key_phrases: Failure to confirm PRS holding drill string before unlatching elevators
Poor communications between Driller and Assistant Driller
Short service employees training on rig floor

threats.name: Driller opens elevators before PRS secures drill pipe
Poor communication between Driller and Assistant Driller
Floor Hand slips on wet/slippery deck during evacuation

consequences.name: Damage to rig equipment (PRS hoist, drill pipe, covers)

controls.name: Work Instructions for Tripping Operations - POOH (WI-TH-DR-OPS-096)
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control requiring confirmation that PRS is holding drill string before unlatching elevators
controls.lod_basis: Primary procedural safeguard to prevent uncontrolled release of suspended load

supporting_text: the Driller was not following the Work Instructions for Tripping Operations Pull Out of Hole (POOH) No. WI-TH-DR-OPS-096
Failure to confirm the PRS is holding the drill string before unlatching the elevators will result in dropping stand and injuring personnel
the Driller did not confirm with the AD that the PRS had secured the stand of pipe before opening the elevators

controls.name: Pipe Racking System (PRS) - Mechanical Load Securing Device
controls.barrier_role: Mechanical device to secure drill pipe before elevator release
controls.lod_basis: Primary mechanical safeguard to prevent load drop

supporting_text: the Assistant Driller (AD) could get the Pipe Racking System (PRS) back into position and grab the 13th stand of 4-1/2 inch drill pipe
the Driller functioned the elevators to the open position. This resulted in the stand of pipe falling

controls.name: Communication Protocol between Driller and Assistant Driller
controls.barrier_role: Procedural communication safeguard to ensure PRS status confirmation
controls.lod_basis: Critical human-to-human communication to verify equipment status before critical action

supporting_text: Poor communications between the Driller and the AD - the Driller did not confirm with the AD that the PRS had secured the stand of pipe before opening the elevators

controls.name: Red Zone / Yellow Zone Designation and Exclusion
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to keep personnel out of hazardous areas during load handling
controls.lod_basis: Secondary safeguard to minimize personnel exposure to falling load hazard

supporting_text: Although the red zone was clear at the time of the event, the stand of pipe fell across the yellow zone of the rig floor within close proximity of the three Floor Hands
the stand in the rotary to fall across rig floor. There were four floor hands on the drill floor at this time that aggressively tried to egress the area

controls.name: Rig Floor Surface Maintenance and Slip Prevention
controls.barrier_role: Engineering/maintenance control to prevent slips during emergency evacuation
controls.lod_basis: Secondary safeguard to prevent secondary injuries during load drop evacuation

supporting_text: one Floor Hand slipping on the deck
The BSEE Investigation Team walked down the egress route in the Red Zone and found the slippery surface where the Injured Person fell

controls.name: Elevator System Inspection and Maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Preventive maintenance to ensure elevator system functions reliably
controls.lod_basis: Primary safeguard to prevent equipment malfunction that could contribute to load drop

supporting_text: one of the witnesses stated that the elevator was having trouble opening up
the witness was told that once they got out of the hole, an effort would be made to see what was causing the problem

controls.name: Supervision and Oversight of Rig Operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure compliance with procedures and safe work practices
controls.lod_basis: Primary safeguard to ensure procedures are followed and unsafe conditions are identified

supporting_text: BSEE was informed the Driller had a similar incident on January 3, 2023
safety culture issues noted during the interviews
one of the experienced Floor Hands was showing two short service employees (SSE) how to operate the Iron Rough Neck

controls.name: Training and Competency Verification for Rig Personnel
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure personnel are competent to perform critical tasks
controls.lod_basis: Primary safeguard to ensure personnel understand and can execute procedures correctly

supporting_text: one of the experienced Floor Hands was showing two short service employees (SSE) how to operate the Iron Rough Neck

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
